title: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup

seo.title: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup: Easy Indian Dessert Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make delicious Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup at home! This authentic Indian dessert recipe features soft milk solids soaked in fragrant rose syrup. Perfect for festivals!

seo.imageAlt: A plate of warm, soft Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup garnished with pistachios and rose petals.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the aromatic delight of homemade Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup, a classic Indian dessert.

description: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup is an iconic Indian dessert featuring soft, spongy fried milk-solids soaked in a fragrant rose-infused sugar syrup. These delightful dumplings offer a rich, sweet flavor with delicate floral notes that melt in your mouth. It is a perfect treat for special occasions and festivals, cherished across India for its comforting warmth and exquisite taste.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sugar Syrup
instructions.text: In a wide pan, combine sugar and water. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves completely. Add crushed cardamom pods and saffron strands (if using). Simmer for 5-7 minutes until the syrup becomes slightly sticky, but not a thick one-string consistency. Remove from heat and stir in the rose water. Keep the syrup warm.

instructions.title: Mix Dry Ingredients for Jamuns
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine milk powder, all-purpose flour, and baking soda. Mix well to ensure everything is evenly distributed.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add melted ghee to the dry ingredients and rub it in gently with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gradually add warm milk, one tablespoon at a time, and gently bring the mixture together to form a soft, pliable dough. Do not knead aggressively; just mix until combined. The dough should be slightly sticky.

instructions.title: Shape the Gulab Jamuns
instructions.text: Grease your palms lightly with ghee. Take small portions of the dough and gently roll them into smooth, crack-free balls, about 1-inch in diameter. Ensure there are no cracks, as they can break during frying. Repeat with the remaining dough.

instructions.title: Fry the Gulab Jamuns
instructions.text: Heat oil or ghee in a deep pan or kadai over very low heat. The oil should be warm, not hot. To test, drop a tiny piece of dough; it should gently sizzle and rise slowly. Carefully add the shaped jamun balls, frying them in batches to avoid overcrowding. Fry on low heat, turning them occasionally, until they are evenly golden brown all over. This slow frying ensures they cook through and become soft.

instructions.title: Soak and Serve
instructions.text: Once fried, immediately transfer the hot gulab jamuns into the warm sugar syrup. Let them soak for at least 30 minutes to 1 hour, or preferably longer, to absorb the syrup and become soft and plump. Serve warm, garnished with chopped pistachios and dried rose petals. They can also be enjoyed chilled.

tips: Always fry gulab jamuns on very low heat. This allows them to cook evenly from inside out, preventing them from being raw in the center or hard on the outside.
Ensure your sugar syrup is warm, not boiling hot, when adding the fried jamuns. If the syrup is too hot, the jamuns might shrivel. If it's too cold, they won't absorb the syrup properly.
For the softest gulab jamuns, use full-fat milk powder or fresh khoya. Avoid over-kneading the dough, as it can make the jamuns dense or hard.
article: Gulab Jamun Rose Syrup is a beloved Indian Desserts dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
